title: RWA Calculation Accuracy and Traceability
description: Ensure all RWA calculations are accurate, deterministic, and fully traceable with complete audit trail of inputs, parameters, and intermediate results.

regulatory_basis.rationale: Standardized approach requires accurate calculation of risk-weighted exposure amounts
Risk data must be accurate and reliable with validation controls

required_evidence.description: Golden regression test results showing calculation accuracy
Complete calculation trace for sample exposures
Audit log of all calculation executions

acceptance_criteria.description: All golden test cases pass with 100% accuracy
Calculation results are deterministic (same inputs produce same outputs)
Complete audit trail exists for every calculation

test_obligations.description: Golden regression test suite covering all exposure classes
Determinism test running same calculation 100 times

implementation_hints.description: Use Decimal type for all monetary calculations to avoid floating-point errors
implementation_hints.example: from decimal import Decimal; rwa = Decimal('1000.00') * Decimal('0.5')

implementation_hints.description: Separate calculation engine from data access layer for testability
Use pytest with golden test fixtures for regression testing

anti_patterns.risk: Floating-point arithmetic can introduce rounding errors that violate regulatory accuracy requirements
anti_patterns.alternative: Use Decimal type with explicit rounding to required precision

anti_patterns.description: Modifying calculation logic without updating golden tests
anti_patterns.risk: Changes may introduce errors that go undetected
anti_patterns.alternative: Always update golden tests when calculation logic changes and document the reason

bob_actions.description: Block merge if any golden test fails
Require review from risk management team
